---
page-layout: full
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: jolla
  image: assets/profile.png
  links:
    - icon: twitter
      text: Twitter
      href: https://twitter.com/LukasAd85691603
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/lukasadam
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/lukas-adam
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: "mailto:gm.lukas.adam@gmail.com"   
format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <link href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.0.0-beta3/css/all.min.css" rel="stylesheet">
listing:
  id: sample-listings
  contents: posts
  type: grid
  sort: "date desc"
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  grid-columns: 3
---

```{=html}
<div class="polygon-wrapper">
        <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 1440 320">
  <path fill="white" fill-opacity="1" d="M0,64L24,85.3C48,107,96,149,144,154.7C192,160,240,128,288,101.3C336,75,384,53,432,48C480,43,528,53,576,80C624,107,672,149,720,160C768,171,816,149,864,128C912,107,960,85,1008,64C1056,43,1104,21,1152,21.3C1200,21,1248,43,1296,58.7C1344,75,1392,85,1416,90.7L1440,96L1440,320L1416,320C1392,320,1344,320,1296,320C1248,320,1200,320,1152,320C1104,320,1056,320,1008,320C960,320,912,320,864,320C816,320,768,320,720,320C672,320,624,320,576,320C528,320,480,320,432,320C384,320,336,320,288,320C240,320,192,320,144,320C96,320,48,320,24,320L0,320Z"></path>
</svg>      
    </div>
<div><h1 class="polygon-header center">Hey there 👋</h1></div>
```

::: {#hero-heading}
:::

::: {.justify}
Welcome to my corner of the internet! I'm Lukas, a bioinformatician at the [Roche Institute of Human Biology](https://institutehumanbiology.com/) (IHB) in Basel, where I spend most of my days thinking about data, biology, and how to advance complex human model systems, so called organoids.

Organoids are 3D cell culture systems that mimic the structure and function of real organs, and they have the potential to revolutionize our understanding of human biology and disease. My work is focused on developing and applying computational methods to analyze and interpret data from these systems. Over the past few years, I've had the privilege of working on a variety of different projects tackling important bioengineering questions and I've developed a deep appreciation for the power of computational biology to drive scientific discovery.

Beyond research and coding, I love traveling and sharing what I learn along the way. Every now and then, I’ll share some thoughts—and maybe a travel story or two—right here in blog form.

Thanks for stopping by and feel free to reach out! 🚀
:::

<br><hr>

# Here are my latest blog posts {.center}

::: {#sample-listings}
:::
